sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c in ['W', 'M', 'G'] or (c == 'p' and n['pixels'] >= 15):
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  color={c} px={n['pixels']} center=({center_r},{center_c})")

# Check goal pieces
goal_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"\nGoal nodes: {len(goal_nodes)}, total px: {sum(n['pixels'] for n in goal_nodes)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The goal has been "consumed" and converted:
- Goal (G, 6px) now at (48,29) - this is where the white diamond was
- The goal pieces that were at (53,26), (53,30), (54,28) are now white (W)
- Purple (p, 20px) still at (51,28) with magenta at (51,28)
So clicking the purple square when it overlaps the goal:
1. Converts the goal pieces to white
2. Moves the goal to where the white diamond was
3. The purple square stays in place
This is the same swap mechanic I saw before. The goal swaps with the white diamond.
So the pattern is:
- Click ring pixel → white moves there, purple square spawns on path
- Click purple square → white moves there, new purple square spawns closer to goal
- When purple square overlaps goal → goal swaps with white diamond
The goal is to get the purple square to overlap the goal, then click the purple square to swap the goal to the white diamond's position, then advance the purple square to the new goal position, etc.
But wait - the goal moved from (53,28) area to (48,29), which is closer to the purple square at (51,28). Let me click the purple square again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Goal now at (48,29), purple at (51,28). Click purple to advance.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 28}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
